channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: “Democracy for Sale ? : Tshabangu Hands Voters to Zanu PF” @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| Controversial Citizens Coalition for Change leader Sengezo Tshabangu has been accused of political hypocrisy after calling for an... Continue reading "“Democracy for Sale ?: Tshabangu Hands Voters to Zanu PF”" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Citizens Want Jobs Not Amendments, Action Democratic Movement Challenges Zanu PF Regime @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| The Action Democratic Movement has slammed the passage of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3, saying it has deepened... Continue reading "Citizens Want Jobs Not Amendments, Action Democratic Movement Challenges Zanu PF Regime" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Dying for a Russian War: The Tragic Truth About Zimbabweans Fighting on the Frontlines in Ukraine
item.description: Thousands of miles from the bustling markets of Mbare or the quiet suburbs of Bulawayo, a silent and gruesome tragedy is unfolding in the frozen trenches of Eastern Europe. While the global community remains transfixed by the high-stakes geopolitical maneuvering between the Kremlin and the West, young Zimbabwean men are being ground into the mud [...] The post Dying for a Russian War: The Tragic Truth About Zimbabweans Fighting on the Frontlines in Ukraine first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: CAB3 and the myth of electoral peace: Why Zimbabwe’s next parliamentary election could be its most violent @nehandaradio
item.description: In a historical analysis spanning Zimbabwe’s post-independence elections, Dr Phillan Zamchiya contends that CAB3 rests on a false premise, arguing that removing direct presidential elections is unlikely to reduce violence and may instead make future parliamentary contests even more fiercely contested. The post CAB3 and the myth of electoral peace: Why Zimbabwe’s next parliamentary election could be its most violent appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: King Mswati Rewards Mnangagwa Frontman Wicknell Chivayo With Eswatini Diplomatic Passport, Citizenship @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| Public outrage has erupted after the Government of Eswatini revealed that controversial Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo made a... Continue reading "King Mswati Rewards Mnangagwa Frontman Wicknell Chivayo With Eswatini Diplomatic Passport, Citizenship" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mangwana, Matinyarare trade blows over who deserves credit for Zimbabwe sanctions rollback @nehandaradio
item.description: A public war of words has erupted between government spokesman Nick Mangwana and anti-sanctions campaigner Rutendo Matinyarare, with the pair offering sharply contrasting accounts of who deserves credit for the gradual rollback of Western sanctions on Zimbabwe. The post Mangwana, Matinyarare trade blows over who deserves credit for Zimbabwe sanctions rollback appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Tshabangu pushes to scrap by-elections @bulawayo24
item.description: Self-styled Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) secretary-general Sengezo Tshabangu has called for the suspension of parliamentary by-elections between general elections, proposing constitutional chan... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Mthwakazi condemns re-arrest of activist who served jail term @bulawayo24
item.description: The Mthwakazi Republic Party (MRP) has condemned the re-arrest of one of its members over an offence for which it says he has already served a prison sentence, describing the development as "shocking"... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.description: Dynamos FC have clarified that Genesis Mangombe remains the club’s head coach despite his suspension, saying no decision has been made to dismiss him while disciplinary proceedings are underway.The cl... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe name traveling squad for Rugby Nations Cup 2026 @3menonaboat
item.description:
Head coach Pieter Benade has selected a blend of experienced campaigners and emerging talent, with the squad featuring a mix of locally-based players and those plying their trade abroad.

The post Zimbabwe name traveling squad for Rugby Nations Cup 2026 first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: Trials and tribulations of being a foreigner @bulawayo24
item.description: After working hard and earning R200, a mother came home to find a group of women singing loudly "ABAHAMBE." The group yelled at her, demanding that all foreigners leave South Africa by June 30, 2026.T... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: St David’s Bonda Girls’ High School Teacher On The Run Over Student Abuse Allegations
item.description: Married Teacher Flees As Manicaland Boarding School Abuse Scandal Erupts A teacher at St David’s Bonda Girls’ High School has fled his home and gone into hiding after multiple students came forward with harrowing sexual abuse allegations, triggering a manhunt across Manicaland province. Anesu Mautsa, 48, a married father of three, is wanted by the [...] The post St David’s Bonda Girls’ High School Teacher On The Run Over Student Abuse Allegations appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
